item.title: New Jersey cannot regulate Kalshi's prediction market, US appeals court rules
item.description: A federal appeals court on вҖӢMonday ruled that New вҖҢJersey gaming regulators cannot prevent Kalshi from вҖӢallowing people вҖӢin the state to use вҒ its prediction вҖӢmarket to place financial вҖӢbets on the outcome of sporting events.

item.title: BlackRock files for Nasdaq-100 fund, expanding competition with Invesco
item.description: BlackRock has filed for an exchange-traded fund that will track the Nasdaq-100 , in a challenge вҖӢto Invesco's dominance in a market where вҖҢonly a handful of funds directly follow the tech-heavy index.

item.title: Brazil's Embraer CFO Garcia resigns, CEO Gomes Neto to take interim role
item.description: Brazilian вҖҢplanemaker Embraer вҖӢsaid вҖӢon Monday that вҒ CFO вҖӢAntonio вҖӢCarlos Garcia has вҖӢresigned вҖӢand CEO Francisco вҖҢGomes вҒ Neto will assume вҖӢthe вҖӢposition вҒ on вҖӢan вҖӢinterim вҒ basis.

item.title: Investors press Amazon, Microsoft and Google on water, power use in US data centers
item.description: Amazon, Microsoft and Alphabet's Google have each recently abandoned construction of multibillion-dollar data centers over community opposition and now the companies вҖӢare coming under shareholder pressure over the environmental impact of their projects.

item.title: National Healthcare Properties moves closer to public markets with US IPO filing
item.description: Real estate investment trust вҖӢNational Healthcare Properties on вҖҢMonday made public its paperwork for an вҖӢinitial public offering вҖӢin the United States.

item.title: Oracle appoints Hilary Maxson as CFO
item.description: Oracle said вҖӢon Monday it вҖҢhad appointed Hilary Maxson as chief financial officer, effective вҖӢimmediately.

item.title: US equity funds see a second successive weekly inflow
item.description: U.S. equity funds witnessed substantial inflows in the seven days to April 1 as worries вҖӢover the Middle East war eased temporarily after вҖҢPresident Donald Trump indicated that the United States was nearing the completion of its objectives for the war.

item.title: Goldman Sachs private credit fund defies redemption surge across industry
item.description: Goldman Sachs' private credit fund said on Monday investors requested to repurchase just under 5% of shares in the first quarter, staying within its cap and defying a broader rise in redemption requests across the вҖӢsector.

item.title: Neurocrine to buy Soleno Therapeutics for $2.9 billion
item.description: Neurocrine Biosciences will acquire rare disease drugmaker вҖӢSoleno Therapeutics for $2.9 billion in вҖҢcash, the companies said on Monday, marking the neuroscience-focused drugmaker's expansion into вҖӢmetabolic disorders.

item.title: Madison Air targets $13.2 billion valuation in US IPO
item.description: Ventilation and вҖҢfiltration systems provider Madison Air Solutions said on вҖӢMonday it was вҖӢtargeting a valuation of вҒ up to $13.2 billion вҖӢin its initial public вҖӢoffering in the United States.

item.title: JPMorgan's Dimon warns Iran war may drive inflation and interest rates higher
item.description: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon warned on Monday that the war in Iran risks oil and commodity вҖӢprice shocks that could keep inflation sticky and push interest rates higher than the market now expects.

item.title: US crude premiums climb to record levels as Asia, Europe compete for supply
item.description: Spot premiums for U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude have jumped вҖӢto all-time highs as competition between Asian and European refiners for supply heats up вҖҢto replace Middle Eastern oil flows disrupted by the Iran war, industry sources said.

item.title: U.S. auto safety regulator closes probe into Tesla's driver assistance feature
item.description: The National вҖӢHighway вҖҢTraffic Safety вҖӢAdministration вҖӢsaid on Monday вҒ that вҖӢit вҖӢhad closed a вҖӢprobe вҖӢinto Tesla vehicles' "Actually вҖҢSmart вҒ Summon" feature, covering вҖӢroughly вҖӢ2.59 вҒ million вҖӢvehicles.

item.title: US stock futures edge up as investors assess Mideast ceasefire prospects
item.description: U.S. stock index futures inched вҖӢhigher on Monday, after the main indexes marked their biggest weekly jump in four вҖҢmonths in the last session, as investors assessed prospects of an end to the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Global equity funds draw second weekly inflow amid war de-escalation hopes
item.description: Investors bought global equity funds worth a net $15.02 billion in the March 26-April 1 period, logging a second вҖӢstraight week of inflows, on hopes that the U.S.-Israeli war вҖҢwith Iran could soon de-escalate.

item.title: Indian IT firms face subdued fourth quarter as war, AI concerns persist; weak rupee helps earnings
item.description: Top Indian information technology firms are set to report another lacklustre quarter, with revenue and profit seen rising around 10% year-on-year largely on a weaker rupee rather than underlying growth, seven вҖӢbrokerages said.

item.title: Tesla's South Korean sales up more than 300% to 11,134 vehicles in March
item.description: Tesla saw вҖӢits car registrations вҖҢin South Korea rise вҖӢ330% вҖӢto 11,134 vehicles in вҒ March вҖӢfrom year earlier, вҖӢmarket researcher Carisyou said on вҖӢMonday.

item.title: Citigroup pushes back Fed rate cut timeline after strong job numbers
item.description: Citigroup has pushed back its Fed rate-cut timeline, citing unexpectedly strong U.S. вҖӢjob gains and persistent inflation risks.

item.title: India's Wipro rises after deal to acquire Olam GroupвҖҷs IT business
item.description: Shares of Wipro rose as much as 3.2% on Monday after the Indian IT вҖӢservices firm agreed to buy the IT services business вҖҢof Singapore-based Olam Group for an enterprise value of $375 million.

item.title: UPS, Teamsters reach settlement that caps driver severance offers
item.description: United Parcel Service said on Sunday that it had вҖӢreached an agreement with the вҖҢInternational Brotherhood of Teamsters to cap severance offers at 7,500 drivers after вҖӢa dispute over the вҖӢcompany's plans to cut its workforce.

item.title: Asia markets brace for Trump's promised assault on Iranian infrastructure
item.description: Oil prices rose, bonds fell and stocks were вҖӢmixed at the start of trading in Asia on Monday.

item.title: Dollar steady as traders fret about escalating Iran war
item.description: The dollar was steady, while the yen flirted with the crucial 160 per dollar level.

item.title: How one factory in China learned to live with Trump, tariffs and turmoil
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump's tariffs sought to hurt Chinese manufacturing, but for one electronics maker, a turbulent 2025 ended with a belief that China is a location that is difficult to replicate - as long as things don't change too drastically.

item.title: Gulf funds near deal to back Paramount's $81 billion takeover of Warner, WSJ reports
item.description: Paramount is вҖӢin talks to secure вҒ signed equity вҖӢcommitments of вҖӢclose to $24 billion from three sovereign-wealth вҖӢfunds led вҖӢby Saudi Arabia to вҖҢhelp вҒ back its takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery , вҖӢthe вҖӢWall вҒ Street Journal reported on вҖӢSunday.

item.title: Oil prices open higher as US-Israeli war with Iran continues to disrupt supply
item.description: Oil prices opened higher on вҖӢMonday after the Easter вҖҢholiday weekend as the U.S.-Israeli war with вҖӢIran continues to вҖӢdisrupt global oil supply.

item.title: OPEC+ debates theoretical oil output hike amid Iran war paralysis, sources say
item.description: OPEC+ may approve an oil output increase on Sunday, four sources from the group said, a rise that will largely exist on paper as its key members are unable to raise production due вҖӢto the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran.

item.title: Foxconn first-quarter revenue jumps, company cautions on geopolitics
item.description: Taiwan's вҖӢFoxconn, the world's largest contract electronics maker, reported a 29.7% вҖҢon-year rise in first-quarter revenue on strong demand for artificial intelligence products, though it cautioned about "volatile" global politics.

item.title: War weighs on Egypt's private sector as PMI hits near two-year low in March
item.description: Egypt's non-oil private sector deteriorated at its sharpest pace in almost two years in March, as the Middle East wardrove вҖӢup costs and dampened client demand, a closely watched вҖҢbusiness survey showed on Sunday.

item.title: Britain woos expansion effort by Anthropic after US defence clash, FT says
item.description: Britain is trying вҖӢto tempt Anthropic вҖҢto expand its presence вҖӢin вҖӢthe country, as it вҒ seeks вҖӢto capitalise on вҖӢa fight between the maker вҖӢof artifical вҖӢintelligence app Claude вҖҢand вҒ the U.S. defence department, the Financial вҖӢTimes вҖӢsaid вҒ on Sunday.

item.title: Striking JBS workers to return to Colorado plant on promise of talks
item.description: Workers of the world's largest meat company, JBS , agreed to return to work at a beef plant in Greely, Colorado after вҖӢit agreed to resume negotiations, bringing a three-week picket to вҖҢan end, their union said in a statement on Saturday.
